category_title: Preschoolers with expressive language difficulties who use adults as living auxiliary tools
minimal_context: Maliha is sitting in an imaginary spaceship tent during circle time and is asked to describe what she sees.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Maliha, look out from the spaceship tent. Tell us what you see.

STUDENT: I saw sta-ar.

TUTOR: Okay, you saw a star. Tell me more about it.

STUDENT: …ig stars. (shows with hands)

TUTOR: What else do you see out there?

STUDENT: (Stutters and repeats sounds, appearing to struggle to find appropriate words) Mummy and daddy... (continues to show with hand gestures)

options.A: Take the student to another room away from the circle time group in order to calm her down.
options.B: Respond to the student by saying, 'Another star already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Keep going!'
options.C: Ask questions that provide specific choices or options (e.g., 'big stars or little stars?') to support her response.
options.D: Immediately remove all demands and the spaceship activity materials for 60 seconds during the circle time session.
